plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit leveraged ETFs and reduce risk. Increase QQQI to 25% for income and lower volatility. Buy NOW at 5% for a top-quality addition. Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration. Build a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and stability. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and building a substantial cash buffer, leading to lower potential drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and meet mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit underperforming leveraged ETFs. Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration. Increase QQQI to 22% for income and balance. Buy ANET at 5% for quality tech exposure. Add TECL at 3% for focused tech leverage. Maintain a 10% cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for about 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing some high-quality tech names, offering a moderate risk-reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit underperforming leveraged ETFs. Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration. Buy NOW at 8% for high-score, strong risk/reward. Add ANET at 7% for high-score quality tech. Buy TECL at 7% for high-score leveraged tech exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It leans into high-scoring setups and leveraged tech, which significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay and rebalancing effects of leveraged ETFs, alongside strict stop discipline to manage higher risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit leveraged ETFs and reduce risk. Sell CRWD to exit high-beta single name. Trim SMH to 5% to reduce sector concentration. Increase QQQI to 35% for a strong income focus. Maintain a 20% cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It rotates heavily into income-focused ETFs and maintains a large cash buffer, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
